summary:
Socioeconomic disparities in mortality persist after retirement due to ongoing barriers such as limited access to preventative care, poor insurance coverage, and inadequate mental health services. The data supports continued heightened risks among lower-income individuals post-retirement, particularly related to substance use disorders and premature death through preventable causes. 

Note: This summary is based solely on the provided information; no external knowledge was used beyond what’s presented here.

Okay, let me now refine it strictly within two sentences focusing only on core clinical facts:

Socioeconomic disparities in mortality endure after retirement because retired individuals from lower income groups face worse outcomes tied to chronic diseases, delays in diagnosis, and lack of consistent medical follow-up. Evidence shows these gaps are rooted not just in age but also in systemic inequities impacting prevention strategies and treatment accessibility during early retirement years.
